items.title: GOF launches task forces to curb petrol smuggling on Malaysia-Thailand border
items.summary: TUMPAT: The General Operations Force (GOF) has set up four special task forces to strengthen Ops Tiris 3.0 and tackle petrol smuggling along the Malaysia-Thailand border. GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the units were deployed to target syndicates involved in illegal movements of subsidised fuel. "Besides petrol, the task forces will also focus on other subsidised goods," he said.

items.title: Penang LRT hits milestone with first concrete segment cast in Kulim
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 4 — The Penang Mutiara Line LRT project has achieved a significant milestone with the segmental box girder (SBG) casting yard in Sungai Ular, Kulim now 90 per cent complete. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said in a statement today that the 15.378-hectare facility is set to play a critical role in constructing the project’s elevated guideway by producing the concrete SBG segments that form its main structure. SRS LRT project director Adil Putra Ahmad said the facility will produce approximately 8,800 SBG segments required for the Civil Main Contractor 1 (CMC1) package, which covers a 23.7-kilometre alignment from Silicon Island to Komtar.

items.title: Sebatik Island faces severe water shortage as dam nears collapse
items.summary: TAWAU: Sebatik Island is now on the brink of a severe water crisis. Residents said they have been forced to take matters into their own hands by constructing tube wells as an alternative water source. He said the Sabah State Water Department has implemented scheduled water rationing and is distributing treated clean water to households using water tankers.

items.title: Women turn hot spell into profitable sun-dried fish business
items.summary: PONTIAN: The prolonged dry spell has proven a boon for the fishing community in Kampung Nelayan Pontian Besar Kiri, who are taking advantage of the intense heat to produce sun-dried fish. Assisted by one worker, Asmah's husband, Mohd Faizal Abdullah, 76, used to be a fisherman but had to stop six years ago due to a nerve condition. Alhamdulillah, the hot weather produces higher quality dried fish and directly boosts our earnings," she said.

items.title: Man detained after erratic public behaviour disrupts traffic
items.summary: IPOH: Police have detained a man who was behaving erratically and disrupting traffic in Langkap, near Teluk Intan, yesterday. Hilir Perak police chief Assistant Commissioner Chua Kok Lian said a report was received at 9.35pm regarding a naked man acting indecently and obstructing traffic along Jalan Langkap–Chui Chak. "Acting on the information, police detained the 41-year-old man, as seen in videos circulating on social media.

items.title: Umno branch meetings set tone ahead of Johor polls
items.summary: PONTIAN: The 2,380 Johor Umno branches currently holding their annual meetings may be doing so for the last time before the next state election. Johor Umno liaison committee deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said the current Barisan Nasional (BN) state government's mandate is expected to end in April 2027. He said the meetings provide a platform for branch chiefs to brief members on the state government's achievements.

items.title: JB cops searching for Rohingya girl, 11, missing since March 20
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Police have launched a search for an 11-year-old Rohingya girl who has been missing for more than two weeks ago. Johor Baru (North) police chief Assistant Commissioner Radin Ramlan Radin Taha said efforts were ongoing to trace Yasmin Kefayatullah, who was last seen on March 20. He said the girl was last seen at her home in No.

items.title: Communications Ministry sec-gen visits media personnel at Perak Madani Rakyat Programme
items.summary: IPOH: Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah today spent time with media personnel at the Media Centre of the Perak edition of the Madani Rakyat Programme (PMR) 2026, currently taking place at Bulatan Sultan Azlan Shah, Ipoh. Also present were Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) managing director Abdul Karim Fakir Ali, MCMC chief officer of state affairs Bukhari Yahya, Perak MCMC director Mohd Irwan Mohamad, and Perak Information Department director Azrul Hassan. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to officiate the programme today.

items.title: Jertih home fire leaves family with severe burns, neighbour among first responders
items.summary: Jertih Fire and Rescue Station chief Mustafa Ismail said both victims, identified as Fauziah Ali, 75, and her son Azli Abu, 45, were sent by members of the public to the Besut Hospital for treatment. Meanwhile, the victims’ neighbour, Mohd Rafain Ruzlan, 30, said he heard Azli calling for help around 5 am. Azli was trying to extinguish the fire.

items.title: Naked man disrupting traffic in Langkap detained for psychiatric evaluation
items.summary: IPOH, April 4 — Police detained a man who was behaving erratically and disrupting traffic in the town of Langkap, near Teluk Intan, yesterday. Hilir Perak police chief ACP Chua Kok Lian said a report was received at 9.35 pm about a naked man acting indecently and obstructing traffic along Jalan Langkap–Chui Chak. “Acting on the information, police detained the 41-year-old man as seen in videos circulating on social media.

items.title: Blood clots, burning eyes: Pollution chokes north Thailand
items.summary: "It's suffocating," the 36-year-old coconut seller told AFP in Chiang Mai, a tourist destination among the areas affected by dangerous pollution levels this week. Seasonal agricultural burning, forest fires and weather patterns produce an annual pollution season across much of Southeast Asia. This week Thailand's second city, Chiang Mai, regularly topped the IQAir monitor website's most polluted big cities list.

items.title: Terengganu work-from-home plan still under evaluation, no decision yet
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, April 4 — The Terengganu State Government is assessing the need to implement a work from home (WFH) arrangement for its civil servants in light of the global energy crisis arising from conflicts in West Asia. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said the proposal is still under evaluation, and no decision has been finalised. He said the state government is carefully considering the need for such an arrangement, taking into account differing conditions between states.

items.title: Anwar to make maiden Pengerang visit as PM, engaging felda settlers
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will make his inaugural visit to Pengerang tomorrow, marking his first official engagement in the area since taking office. The visit will commence at Dewan Semai Bakti, Felda Semenchu, with a programme involving Felda settlers. Johor Agriculture, Agro-based Industry and Rural Development Committee chairman Datuk Zahari Sarip will represent Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi at the event.

items.title: Residents fear black panther after sheep carcass found near Gua Musang Hospital quarters
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, April 4 — About 30 families living in the Gua Musang Hospital quarters are worried about their safety after a carcass of a sheep, belonging to a resident, was found 10 metres from their premises on Thursday. One of the residents, Mohd Helmiey Shudi Hussin, 42, said that he had been living in the quarters for six years, and this incident was a first. Meanwhile, Kelantan Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) director Wan Mohd Adib Wan Yusoh, when contacted, said that his department would obtain further information regarding the incident.

items.title: Fuel smugglers enlarge tanks to evade detection, says Armizan
items.summary: TUMPAT: Petrol smugglers are adapting their methods, including modifying vehicle tanks to increase capacity for illicit petrol. Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said syndicates have enlarged vehicle tanks to store subsidised Malaysian petrol. The petrol is then smuggled into Thailand, where prices are much higher, making the practice highly lucrative.

items.title: Media veterans recall Dr Ling's warmth, leadership and lasting legacy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Behind his role as a long-serving minister, Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik was a leader known for his warmth and openness, even welcoming members of the media into his home, recalled Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) Chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai. Wong, a prominent media figure and National Journalism Laureate who covered Dr Ling for decades, described the former MCA president as a "soft-spoken but firm" politician who maintained a close relationship with the press corps throughout his career. Dr Ling died today aged 83, leaving behind his wife, Toh Puan Ong Ee Nah, and their two sons, Ling Hee Leong and Ling Hee Keat.

items.title: Indonesia's Mount Dukono erupts, sends ash 1,400m high
items.summary: JAKARTA: Mount Dukono in eastern Indonesia erupted on Saturday, sending an ash column up to 1,400m above its summit. An officer at the Dukono Volcano Observation Post (PGA), Bambang Sugiono, said the eruption occurred at approximately 11.08am local time. He said Mount Dukono, which rises 1,087m above sea level, remains at Alert Level II (Waspada), indicating above-normal volcanic activity.

items.title: Suspected panther attack sparks fear among Gua Musang residents
items.summary: GUA MUSANG: About 30 families living in the Gua Musang Hospital quarters fear for their safety after the carcass of a sheep, believed to have been attacked by a black panther, was found just 10m from their homes on Thursday. Resident Mohd Helmiey Shudi Hussin, 42, said he had lived in the quarters for six years and had never encountered such an incident before. Meanwhile, Kelantan Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) director Wan Mohd Adib Wan Yusoh said it would gather further information on the incident.

items.title: Anwar says large projects on hold amid rising costs, but rakyat-focused initiatives will continue
items.summary: IPOH, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim says several large development projects will be postponed due to rising costs caused by the ongoing conflict in West Asia, but projects directly benefiting the rakyat will continue. Anwar also instructed ministers to temporarily refrain from overseas travel, citing soaring costs. “The Prime Minister also said that even when fuel is available, there is no guarantee flights can proceed because aircraft may need to stop at multiple airports to refuel,” Anwar said.

items.title: Senegal bans government travel as Iran war oil shock hits public finances
items.summary: DAKAR: Senegal's government has suspended all non-essential foreign travel by ministers and top officials, warning of "extremely difficult" times ahead as the US-Israeli conflict with Iran drives global oil prices higher, straining the nation's budget. Addressing a youth event in the coastal town of Mbour on Friday evening, Senegal's Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko pointed to oil trading at about US$115 a barrel, nearly twice the US$62 per barrel assumed in Senegal's budget projections. Sonko cited such measures as justification for debt-laden Senegal's own steps.

items.title: Anwar urges Malaysians to stay grateful, united amid economic growth and global pressures
items.summary: IPOH, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Malaysians to remain grateful and united as the country continues to post strong economic growth despite global uncertainties. Unemployment is very low,” he said. Even so, he said Malaysia remains fortunate compared with some neighbouring countries.

items.title: Minor 3.1-magnitude quake hits off Batu Pahat coast
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A minor 3.1-magnitude earthquake struck off the coast of Batu Pahat this afternoon. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the tremor occurred at 1.54pm, about 42km south of Batu Pahat, at a depth of 10km. MetMalaysia said it would continue monitoring the situation.

items.title: 60 top SPM students in Kedah offered trip to Japan, Indonesia
items.summary: KUALA NERANG: Sixty students in Padang Terap who scored excellent results in the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) will be rewarded with educational trips to Japan and Indonesia. Its chairman, Datuk Zuraidi Rahim, said the students were from two schools adopted by the NGO, as well as seven other schools in the district. "For students from the adopted schools, I will take 20 of them on an educational visit to Japan, while 40 excellent students from the other schools will be taken to Jakarta, Indonesia," he said.

items.title: Raid exposes illegal settlements with surau, shop and subsidised goods in Setia Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A total of 214 undocumented migrants were found living in three illegal settlements concealed within an oil palm plantation behind a hilly area in Setia Alam here. The settlements were uncovered during a midnight Op Bersepadu Mega conducted by the Immigration Department, with the assistance of police and several other enforcement agencies. Selangor Immigration director Khairrul Aminus Kamaruddin said the three settlements were believed to have existed for between five and nine years.

items.title: Johor eyes 1,000 high-skilled jobs from RM700mil China investment
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor is targeting more than 1,000 high-skilled jobs under a proposed RM700 million expansion by China-based GSP Automotive Group Wenzhou Co Ltd. Menteri Besar Johor Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said the potential investment, expected to materialise by 2030, would anchor job opportunities in high-technology manufacturing, including battery systems and robotics. "Johor continues to attract high-quality investments through strategic engagements such as this with GSP Automotive Group. "The state government is firm that every job created must prioritise 'anak Johor' (Johor-born).

items.title: No food price hikes while reserves last, says Mat Sabu
items.summary: Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said the country's food supply is stable and not facing shortages. Mohamad, popularly known as Mat Sabu, said price stability depends on existing national reserves. "Prices will hold while old stock lasts, but may rise when new supplies come in at higher prices."

items.title: Search continues for 3 feared drowned in Pahang
items.summary: KUANTAN: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for three people feared drowned in separate incidents at Pantai Sepat here and Kampung Durian Hijau in Jerantut is continuing today. Pahang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said the search for fisherman Mohd Ishak Ismail, 63, who went missing at Pantai Sepat, has entered its third day. Meanwhile, Mohd Salahuddin said efforts to locate Muhammad Amirul Ashraf Abdullah and Muhammad Danial Haikal Shamsudin, both aged 14, who went missing while bathing in Sungai Pahang at Kampung Durian Hijau, have also been intensified.

items.title: Dzulkefly: Private hospitals pledge support for MHIT base plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Private hospitals have pledged support for the voluntary Malaysia Health and Insurance/Takaful (MHIT) base plan, set to launch in January 2027. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said it is a whole-of-nation effort to improve affordability and access in private healthcare. "At a meeting convened by the Minister of Health and Minister of Finance II in Putrajaya yesterday, private hospitals expressed their support for the base MHIT plan," he told the New Straits Times.

items.title: Traders report stable vegetable prices despite cost pressures
items.summary: Traders said prices from farm suppliers remain reasonable despite rising operating costs following the diesel price hike. Prices remain stable for red and green chillies, which are sold at RM11 to RM12 per kg, Japanese mustard greens (sawi Jepun) at RM8 per kg, water spinach (kangkung) at RM6 per kg, and mustard greens (sawi) at around RM8 per kg, he said. He said cabbage is still sold at RM8 per kg, round spinach at RM6.60 per kg, and tomatoes at about RM2 per kg.

items.title: Anwar: State leaders entitled to salaries, my decision not to take pay is personal choice
items.summary: IPOH, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said it is incorrect to argue that Menteri Besar, ministers or other leaders should forgo their salaries simply because he has chosen not to take his own since assuming office. “Some people say, ‘If the Prime Minister does not take his salary, why should a Menteri Besar take theirs?’ That argument is not correct. Their salaries are their rights,” he said.

items.title: Johor allocates RM1.85mil to support Sikh community
items.summary: PONTIAN: The Johor government has announced a RM1.85 million allocation to support Sikh institutions and community development across the state. The announcement was made during a Vaisakhi luncheon at Gurdwara Sahib Pontian, which also marked the temple's 50th anniversary and drew Sikh organisations and community leaders from across Johor. Additional funding includes RM200,000 for the Johor Sikhs Sports Club, and RM100,000 each for the Malaysian Sikh Union (Johor branch) and Johor Sikh Association.

items.title: China raises concerns over Israel's death penalty law targeting Palestinians
items.summary: BEIJING: China called for Palestinians' rights to be protected after Israel approved a bill to allow the execution of Palestinians convicted on terror charges for deadly attacks. "The legal rights of the Palestinian people should be respected and protected," China's foreign ministry spokeswoman Mao Ning said when asked about the bill at a press briefing. The United Nations said on Tuesday that applying the new bill in occupied Palestinian territory would constitute a war crime.

items.title: Melaka takes WRUR to parliament level, starts in Alor Gajah
items.summary: JASIN: The Melaka government is strengthening the Wakil Rakyat Untuk Rakyat (WRUR) programme by expanding it to the parliamentary level, beginning in Alor Gajah this year to enhance service delivery. "The programme begins in Asahan from April 4 to 11, followed by Gadek (April 12–18) and Machap Jaya (April 19 - 25)," he told reporters after attending the Melaka Sayang Rakyat Aidilfitri celebration here today. Earlier, he launched the WRUR programme, which Asahan assemblyman Datuk Fairul Nizam Roslan also attended.

items.title: Wee Ka Siong pays tribute to Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik, calls him towering statesman and nation builder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong today paid tribute to the late Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik, describing him as a towering statesman, nation builder, and extraordinary son of Malaysia. In a Facebook post, Wee said Dr Ling’s life was defined by resilience, intellect, and an unwavering sense of duty, noting that the former MCA president had devoted his life to public service and nation building. Trained as a medical doctor, Dr Ling made the decision to leave the profession to enter politics, believing public service could uplift millions of lives, Wee added.

items.title: King, Queen extend condolences over Liong Sik's passing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, King and Queen of Malaysia, gave their condolences over the passing of former MCA president and transport minister Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik today. Earlier, MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong confirmed the news of his passing this morning. Other political leaders have also shared tributes in honour of Dr Ling.

items.title: King and Queen express condolences on passing of Ling Liong Sik
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, King and Queen of Malaysia have expressed their condolences on the passing of former Transport Minister and MCA Life Honorary President Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik, who died this morning. Dr Ling, 83, who was also a former MCA president, was among the longest-serving veteran politicians in the Cabinet, including holding the Transport Ministry portfolio, in addition to leading the MCA for 17 years. During that period, he held various key positions in the government, including Parliamentary Secretary, Deputy Information Minister, Deputy Finance Minister, and Deputy Education Minister.

items.title: WHO urges urgent health support in Middle East conflict areas
items.summary: GENEVA: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Friday in a social media post that urgent support is needed for the health systems of five West Asia countries affected by the conflict — Lebanon, Iran, Iraq, Syria and Jordan, reported Xinhua. Health systems in the affected countries are "under severe strain" following weeks of intensified violence, Tedros said, adding that the WHO has launched a US$30.3 million flash appeal to support its health response to the escalating conflict in West Asia, with a focus on the five countries. It aims to sustain essential health services and trauma care, strengthen disease surveillance and early warning systems, enhance mass casualty management, and improve national readiness for chemical, biological, radiological and nuclear emergencies, Tedros noted.

items.title: Police arrest two in investigation into Australian fugitive
items.summary: MELBOURNE: Australian police said they arrested two people on Saturday as part of an investigation into how a fugitive evaded capture for seven months after he had killed two police officers. Police resources were poured into the search for Freeman after he shot and killed two officers who had come to search his rural home in Victoria. Victoria Police said a man and woman were arrested on Saturday morning at two separate properties in northeast Victoria and would be interviewed.

items.title: Anwar urges values-led education, warns against 'excellence without a soul'
items.summary: KUALA KANGSAR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for an education system that balances knowledge with values, warning against producing graduates who are intellectually capable but lack conscience. Speaking to students at the Malay College Kuala Kangsar (MCKK), he said privilege must come with accountability and moral responsibility. Education must not ignore ethics, values and character," he said.

items.title: Artemis astronauts more than halfway to Moon, putting Earth in rearview
items.summary: HOUSTON: The Artemis 2 astronauts have passed the halfway point between Earth and the Moon on Saturday as they sped toward a planned lunar flyby, with NASA releasing initial images of Earth taken from inside the Orion spacecraft. "We can see the Moon out of the docking hatch right now, it is a beautiful sight," Koch said around 11 pm (0400 GMT) Friday, according to the space agency's official live broadcast. NASA's online dashboard showed early Saturday that the Orion spacecraft carrying the astronauts was more than 229,000 kilometres (142,000 miles) from Earth.

items.title: Fahmi Fadzil leads tributes as ex-MCA president Ling Liong Sik dies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik’s dedication, leadership and decades of service in government will be long remembered and continue to inspire future generations, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. Dr Ling, who was also MCA life honorary president, died this morning aged 83. Fahmi said Dr Ling made significant contributions to Malaysia’s development throughout his career, including serving as Deputy Information Minister from 1976 to 1982.

items.title: Anwar calls for unity over political point-scoring as global energy crunch bites
items.summary: KUALA KANGSAR, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he is open to discussions with the opposition and any parties willing to help the government find solutions to address the current global energy crisis. “The economic crisis is is beyond our control, although at times the opposition’s rhetoric may sound sophisticated. The entire world is affected by the crisis – not only the oil and gas crisis, but also the broader economic crisis.

items.title: MOHE to announce cost-of-living aid for university students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) is currently studying appropriate measures to assist the campus community, particularly students, in coping with the potential rise in the cost of living following the worsening global energy crisis. Its Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said the crisis has had a significant impact not only on the global economy but has also affected financial management among staff, academics, and students in higher education institutions. Zambry said that yesterday he received a visit from several representatives of the National Student Consultative Council (MPPK) to discuss steps that the ministry and the government can take to help the campus community cope with possible increases in the cost of living, including food prices and daily necessities.

items.title: Anwar vows to prioritise Malaysia’s schools and healthcare amid energy crisis pressures
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said this is to ensure that the quality of education for future generations is not affected. “Our current stance is to ensure that projects (educational facilities) are approved and implemented immediately. However, smaller projects, especially in education and healthcare, will be prioritised.

items.title: Former MCA president Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik dies aged 82
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — Former Transport Minister and MCA Honorary Life President Tun Dr Ling Liong Sik passed away this morning at the age of 83. MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong confirmed the news to Bernama when contacted today. “We received the sad news of Tun Dr Ling’s passing today.

items.title: Neighbours could hear her scream but couldn’t help: Paralysed woman dies trapped in Nilai house
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 4 — A paralysed woman died after being trapped in a fire that destroyed a terrace house in Taman Mantin Baharu here yesterday. A Negeri Sembilan Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) spokesperson said a report on the incident involving the 32-year-old woman was received at 11.33 pm. “The fire involved a terrace house measuring 20 by 65 feet, which was completely destroyed.

items.title: Police hunt driver involved in hit-and-run that killed Putrajaya motorcyclist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — Police are actively hunting for a car driver believed to be involved in a hit-and-run that killed a motorcyclist in Lebuh Saujana, Putrajaya, on Thursday. The victim, Saiful Adli Azmi, 42, also known as Epul Azmi Goreng Pisang, was riding a Yamaha YZF motorcycle and was pronounced dead while on the way to Hospital Putrajaya. Putrajaya district police chief ACP Aidi Sham Mohamed said the district police control centre received reports of the incident at about 11.50pm.

items.title: Silat over selfies — PM tells youth to trade smartphone screens for sparring
items.summary: KUANTAN, April 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim wants the martial art of silat to be strengthened, especially among young people, as it not only builds character and self-discipline but also instils a spirit of struggle. “This spirit must be cultivated. Also present were Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail, Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, and the Prime Minister’s Political Secretary Datuk Ahmad Farhan Fauzi.

items.title: After passenger falls onto LRT track at Abdullah Hukum station, Loke issues urgent reminder to please mind the gap
items.summary: Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook issued the reminder following the incident involving a passenger falling onto the tracks of the Kelana Jaya Light Rail Transit (LRT) line at the Abdullah Hukum Station yesterday evening. “I was informed of an incident involving a passenger who fell onto the LRT tracks at the Abdullah Hukum Station this evening, which led to the activation of the Platform Intrusion Emergency Stop (PIEC) safety system as a train approached the station. Earlier, Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd (Rapid Rail) said that the Kelana Jaya Line LRT service, which was disrupted at 6.09 pm after a passenger fell on the tracks at the Abdullah Hukum Station, was restored at 6.59 pm.

items.title: After Wangsa Maju controversy, Fahmi tells PKR members not to go around ‘checking licences’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — PKR will never protect any party leader or member found to be abusing power, said PKR Information Chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also the PKR president, had issued a stern warning that no one, including ministers, would be shielded if involved in abuse of power. “Never take advantage to abuse power, don’t seize opportunities or take anything.

items.title: Midnight inferno claims four homes and a Toyota Hilux in Kota Bharu
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 4 — Four wooden houses, a motorcycle and a Toyota Hilux were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Chabang Tiga, Kedai Buloh here last night. “A total of 34 personnel, including from the Volunteer Fire Brigade (PBS), were deployed to the scene involving the Kota Bharu, Kota Darul Naim, Wakaf Bharu and Pengkalan Chepa fire and rescue stations, and the fire was brought under control at 11.03 pm,” the statement said. Meanwhile, Kota Bharu Fire and Rescue Station chief Zuzuki Safei, when contacted by Bernama, said the fire did not involve any casualties or loss of life.

items.title: Two teens feared drowned after being swept away by currents in Jerantut river
items.summary: Pahang Fire and Rescue Department public relations officer Zulfadli Zakaria said the department received an emergency call from members of the public at 4.49pm regarding the incident. “A team and fire engine from the Jerantut Fire and Rescue Station were deployed to the location for search and rescue (SAR) operations,” he was quoted as saying. According to Sinar Harian, the SAR operation is being assisted by the Water Rescue Team (PPDA) from the Bukit Angin Fire and Rescue Station in Temerloh.

items.title: Fahmi: MCMC took down 159 fake news posts in four days amid energy crisis misinformation
items.summary: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the false content involved claims about petrol price hikes, allegations that the government was “paying toll” in the Strait of Hormuz, and increases in electricity tariffs by Tenaga Nasional Berhad. He said the public must remain cautious and vigilant regarding information being circulated, as spreading false information or fake news can lead to legal action. Meanwhile, Fahmi, who is also PKR information chief, said party leaders and members must stand together with the government by helping to explain the current situation to the public following the global crisis.

items.title: Man detained after viral video shows kitten being flung against wall at Kajang laundry
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 4 — The Selangor Department of Veterinary Services (DVS) has detained a man suspected of abusing a kitten in an incident at a self-service laundrette in the Kajang area near here. Selangor DVS said in a statement that the man, believed to be in his 40s, was detained after the department was deployed to the scene at about 11 am yesterday to conduct an inspection. The statement said the action followed information and public complaints after a 59-second video went viral on social media, showing a man abusing a kitten by repeatedly throwing it against a wall and onto a mattress at a self-service laundrette.

items.title: Small stalls, big impact: Why Anwar wants to focus on local traders over mega projects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — The government has taken precautionary measures in coordinating the implementation of national policies, particularly to address the impact of the global energy crisis, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said this issue was emphasised at the Kuantan District Development Meeting during his one-day working visit to the district yesterday. Anwar said that, in addition, national defence and security have also been strengthened to ensure the well-being of the government and the people.

items.title: Selangor PAS Youth urges govt to restrict alcohol sales, says fines alone not enough to curb drunk driving
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — Selangor PAS Youth has urged the government to impose stricter restrictions on alcohol sales instead of relying solely on fines to address the rising issue of drunk driving. According to Sinar Harian, Selangor PAS Youth deputy chief Aubidullah Fahim Ibrahim said while the movement fully supports proposed amendments to Section 44 of the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333), requiring offenders to compensate victims’ families is still insufficient. He said penalties in the form of fines alone would not deliver a meaningful deterrent, especially when lives are lost due to drivers operating vehicles under the influence of alcohol.

items.title: War in Iran, economic shockwaves in Southeast Asia
items.summary: Malaysia is not a pure energy importer — Petronas ensures the country has upstream production capacity, and higher crude prices boost government revenue. Asean under pressure Across Southeast Asia, the war has exposed dangerously thin energy reserves. The Philippines, Singapore, Thailand and Vietnam rely on imports for 65 to 95 per cent of their crude supply.

items.title: When one Malayan tapir dies, what else is lost?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 4 — The Malayan tapir is one of Malaysia’s most recognisable forest animals — and one of its most vulnerable. In recent years, tapir roadkill has emerged as a recurring issue across multiple states, particularly in areas where highways cut through or sit near forest reserves. Under the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 [Act 716], there are no specific provisions for accidental killing of protected wildlife by vehicles; mandatory wildlife crossings such as eco-viaducts or underpasses; enforceable speed limits in wildlife corridors; and required mitigation after repeated roadkill incidents.

items.title: Man loses RM1.3m after ‘law firm’ allegedly releases deposit in fake land deal
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 3 — A company manager lost more than RM1.3 million after allegedly falling victim to a fraudulent land sale scheme in Kulai earlier this year. Kulai police chief ACP Tan Seng Lee said a report on the incident was lodged on January 8 by a 42-year-old man. Initial investigations found that the victim’s company, through its appointed lawyer, had expressed interest in purchasing a property and subsequently paid a deposit to a law firm purportedly representing the seller.

items.title: Police arrest 67 in raids on 13 illegal gambling dens across Selangor and Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 3 — Police arrested 67 individuals in raids conducted at 13 premises suspected of conducting illegal gambling in Selangor and Sabah from Wednesday until yesterday. “The 13 premises include eight in Seri Kembangan and North Klang, Selangor and five premises in Kota Kinabalu, Tawau and Sandakan, Sabah,” he said in a statement here today, adding that various electronic equipment was seized, including nine televisions, tablets (95), laptops (17), controllers/consoles (61), simulator machines (20), computers (38) and RM16,553 in cash. “The suspects are being investigated under Section 4B(a), 4(1)(c) and 6(1) of the Common Gaming Houses Act 1953 as well as Section 6(1)(c) and 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63, he added.
